html:
[204]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 卜疑轩 - A term combining '卜' (divination) and '疑' (doubt), likely referring to a place for reflection on uncertainties or divination.
  • 负才 - Literally 'carrying talent,' meaning to be talented or gifted.
  • 跌宕不羁 - A compound term describing someone who is unrestrained, free-spirited, and unconventional.
  • 狭邪游 - Literally 'narrow alleyways,' referring to visits to brothels or engaging in licentious behavior.
  • 切责 - Means 'severe reprimand' or 'sharp criticism.'
  • 遁不敢归 - Literally 'fled and dared not return,' indicating that the person escaped and was too ashamed or fearful to go back.
  • 驺从甚盛 - Refers to a large retinue of attendants, suggesting the presence of a high-ranking official.
  • 呵叱而来 - Means 'came with shouts and commands,' indicating a commanding or authoritative presence.
  • 閈闳高敞 - Describes a grand and open entrance, suggesting a prestigious or noble residence.
  • 阀阅家也 - Refers to a family of high social status or a noble household.
  • 肃客入 - Means 'to invite the guest in respectfully.'
  • 曲栏洞室 - Describes a complex of winding corridors and rooms, suggesting a well-arranged and spacious interior.
  • 竹林花径 - Refers to bamboo groves and flower-lined paths, indicating a scenic and elegant environment.
  • 西偏小室 - Means 'a small room on the west side,' describing the location of the '卜疑轩.'
  • 系于《易》 - Refers to the *Yijing* (Book of Changes), one of the Five Classics.
  • 杂见于百氏之书 - Means 'seen in various books,' suggesting that the name appears in multiple texts.
  • 逊谢 - Means 'to humbly decline' or 'to show respect by refusing.'
  • 叩问姓名 - Means 'to ask for the name,' indicating a polite inquiry.
  • 竟不告 - Means 'finally did not reveal,' suggesting that the person remained mysterious or evasive.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 褚青，馀杭人 - Refers to a person named Zhu Qing from Yuhang, a place in Zhejiang Province.
  • 山左 - Refers to the area east of the Taihang Mountains, historically a region in northern China.
  • 舅氏马公 - Refers to Zhu Qing's uncle, whose surname is Ma, and who held an official position.
  • 閈闳高敞 - Describes the grand entrance of a noble household, reflecting the social hierarchy of the time.
  • 《诗》 - Refers to the *Shijing* (Book of Poetry), one of the Five Classics.
  • 《易》 - Refers to the *Yijing* (Book of Changes), another of the Five Classics.
  • 百氏之书 - Refers to a wide range of texts, including philosophical, historical, and literary works.

Proper Nouns

  • 褚青 - The name of the protagonist.
  • 馀杭 - The place name Yuhang, located in Zhejiang Province.
  • 山左 - The area east of the Taihang Mountains.
  • 马公 - The name of Zhu Qing's uncle, who held an official position.
  • 《诗》 - The *Shijing* (Book of Poetry), one of the Five Classics.
  • 《易》 - The *Yijing* (Book of Changes), another of the Five Classics.
  • 百氏之书 - A general term for a wide range of texts, including philosophical, historical, and literary works.

[205]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 既而 - Indicates a sequence of events, meaning 'afterward' or 'soon after.'
  • 水陆具陈 - Describes a banquet with a wide array of dishes, both aquatic and terrestrial, laid out.
  • 繁音靡曼 - Refers to complex and delicate music, often with a focus on ornate and intricate melodies.
  • 褚素善音律 - Indicates that Chǔ is skilled in music and rhythm.
  • 佳客在坐，安用旧曲 - A rhetorical question meaning 'With distinguished guests present, why use old tunes?'
  • 妮子辈自制新腔 - Refers to the young women (妮子辈) composing their own new tunes.
  • 不识中听否 - A polite way of asking if the music is pleasing or not.
  • 《九尾醮》、《夜篝红》、《玉面娘》、《绎缯囊儿慢》 - Titles of the new songs, which are likely to be unfamiliar to modern readers.
  • 如孤洞，今夜月华云涌 - A poetic description of a scene, possibly a metaphor for a state of mind or a setting.
  • 东瓜棚侧犬初眠，北斗垣中星欲动 - Describes a scene with a dog sleeping near a gourd trellis and stars in the Big Dipper constellation beginning to move.
  • 你莫西，我莫东 - A playful or flirtatious exchange, possibly indicating a dance or a game.
  • 大家看看，大家拜拜，大家送送 - A series of actions, possibly a ritual or a social interaction.
  • 青翰被，与谁共 - Refers to a blue feathered blanket, asking who it is shared with.
  • 也则待掠鬓梳头，学那些颠鸾倒凤 - Describes preparing for a romantic encounter.
  • 不管小夭娘指尖儿湿破窗缝，睡也么浓；怕则怕，晓鸟数声，啼断一林幽梦 - A poetic description of a dream interrupted by morning birds.
  • 吴王宫柳醉春烟，阿姊腰肢二八年 - Describes a beautiful woman in a poetic setting.
  • 昨夜伴郎郎未惯，今宵珍重向婵娟 - Describes a night of companionship and affection.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 褚 - Likely a historical or fictional character, skilled in music.
  • 主人 - The host of the banquet, possibly a noble or influential figure.
  • 妮子辈 - Refers to young women, possibly courtesans or entertainers.
  • 东瓜棚 - A gourd trellis, a common feature in traditional Chinese gardens.
  • 北斗垣 - The Big Dipper constellation, often used in Chinese poetry and literature.
  • 小夭娘 - A term of endearment, possibly referring to a young woman.
  • 吴王宫 - Refers to the palace of King Wu of Wu, a historical figure from the Spring and Autumn period.
  • 郎 - A term of endearment, often used to address a lover or close companion.

Proper Nouns

  • 褚 - A character skilled in music.
  • 主人 - The host of the banquet.
  • 妮子辈 - Young women, possibly courtesans or entertainers.
  • 《九尾醮》、《夜篝红》、《玉面娘》、《绎缯囊儿慢》 - Titles of the new songs.
  • 小夭娘 - A term of endearment, possibly referring to a young woman.
  • 吴王宫 - The palace of King Wu of Wu.

[206]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 又一女向主人歌曰 - A common narrative device where a character sings to another, introducing a new episode or perspective.
  • 元邱校尉太风魔 - A metaphorical expression where '风魔' means 'mad' or 'crazy', describing the character's behavior.
  • 漫使佳人斗艳歌 - A metaphorical expression where '漫使' means 'arbitrarily' or 'recklessly', and '斗艳歌' means 'compete in singing'.
  • 斗柄栏干天欲曙 - A metaphorical expression where '斗柄' means 'handle of the Big Dipper', '栏干' means 'railings', and '天欲曙' means 'dawn is approaching', describing the time of day.
  • 须防华表照双娥 - A metaphorical expression where '华表' means 'ornamental pillar', and '照双娥' means 'illuminating the two beauties', suggesting a warning or caution.
  • 主人笑曰 - A common narrative device where the host responds with a smile, indicating a reaction to the situation.
  • 褚先生非其人也 - A metaphorical expression where '非其人也' means 'is not the right person', indicating a negative assessment.
  • 先生醉矣，可扶先生睡 - A metaphorical expression where '醉矣' means 'is drunk', and '可扶先生睡' means 'can help the master sleep', indicating a concern for the well-being of the guest.
  • 吾与东城君闲话去矣 - A metaphorical expression where '闲话' means 'casual conversation', and '去矣' means 'leave now', indicating a departure.
  • 诸女引入卧室 - A metaphorical expression where '引入' means 'lead into', and '卧室' means 'bedroom', indicating a transition to a private space.
  • 即拥一姬与狎 - A metaphorical expression where '拥' means 'embrace', '一姬' means 'one of the ladies', and '狎' means 'playful intimacy', indicating a physical interaction.
  • 诸女顾之而笑 - A metaphorical expression where '顾之' means 'look at him', and '而笑' means 'and laugh', indicating a reaction to the situation.
  • 有顷而褚已颓矣 - A metaphorical expression where '有顷' means 'after a while', and '颓矣' means 'exhausted', indicating a change in the guest's state.
  • 诸女以次嬲淫 - A metaphorical expression where '以次' means 'in turn', '嬲' means 'tease', and '淫' means 'lust', indicating a series of interactions.
  • 俱觉梦寐中交融欢洽 - A metaphorical expression where '梦寐中' means 'in a dream', '交融' means 'intertwined', and '欢洽' means 'joyful and harmonious', indicating a state of pleasure.
  • 非复人道之常也 - A metaphorical expression where '非复' means 'no longer', '人道' means 'human nature', and '之常也' means 'usual', indicating a departure from normal behavior.
  • 次日梦觉，体不胜惫 - A metaphorical expression where '梦觉' means 'awaken from a dream', '体不胜惫' means 'body is too weak', indicating a state of exhaustion.
  • 开目瞪视，乃卧丛薄间 - A metaphorical expression where '开目' means 'open eyes', '瞪视' means 'stare', and '卧丛薄间' means 'lying among the grass', indicating a confused state.
  • 宛转细思，盖狐所为也 - A metaphorical expression where '宛转' means 'ponder', '细思' means 'think carefully', and '盖狐所为也' means 'it seems the fox's doing', indicating a realization.
  • 卜疑轩者，狐性善疑也 - A metaphorical expression where '卜疑轩' means 'the Pavilion of Doubt', '狐性' means 'fox nature', and '善疑也' means 'is good at doubting', indicating a characteristic of the fox.
  • 其语言词曲，皆狐隐语也 - A metaphorical expression where '语言词曲' means 'words and phrases', and '皆狐隐语也' means 'are all foxes' hidden meanings', indicating a mysterious quality.
  • 狼狈归舅所，卧不能起者数月 - A metaphorical expression where '狼狈' means 'in a sorry state', '归舅所' means 'return to the uncle's place', and '卧不能起者数月' means 'lying unable to get up for several months', indicating a prolonged period of illness.
  • 亦若公 - A metaphorical expression where '亦若公' means 'also like the gentleman', indicating a comparison.
  • 族祖亦若公，为邑诸生 - A metaphorical expression where '族祖' means 'ancestor', '亦若公' means 'also like the gentleman', and '为邑诸生' means 'was a student in the county', indicating a background.
  • 一日病甚，觉其气自口出 - A metaphorical expression where '一日' means 'one day', '病甚' means 'very ill', and '觉其气自口出' means 'felt his breath coming out of his mouth', indicating a severe illness.
  • 遂离形飞于窗外 - A metaphorical expression where '遂离形' means 'then left his body', and '飞于窗外' means 'flew out of the window', indicating a supernatural event.
  • 但见云霞丽天，长空万里 - A metaphorical expression where '但见' means 'only see', '云霞丽天' means 'clouds and rosy light in the sky', and '长空万里' means 'vast expanse of sky', indicating a visual scene.
  • 意所欲到，身即随之 - A metaphorical expression where '意所欲到' means 'wherever the mind desires', and '身即随之' means 'the body follows', indicating a state of freedom.
  • 飘飘然，泠泠然 - Metaphorical expressions where '飘飘然' means 'light and airy', and '泠泠然' means 'cool and refreshing', indicating a state of being.
  • 乘云御风不逾其乐也 - A metaphorical expression where '乘云御风' means 'ride the clouds and control the wind', and '不逾其乐也' means 'cannot surpass the joy', indicating a state of bliss.
  • 既而身在极西，见阳乌入于虞渊 - A metaphorical expression where '既而' means 'then', '身在极西' means 'in the far west', '见阳乌' means 'see the sunbird', and '入于虞渊' means 'enter the abyss of the sun', indicating a location and a celestial event.
  • 日暮徘徊，欲归不识路 - A metaphorical expression where '日暮' means 'sunset', '徘徊' means 'linger', and '欲归不识路' means 'want to return but don't know the way', indicating a state of confusion.
  • 忽有数鸟东还，因与俱飞，疾乃倍于鸟 - A metaphorical expression where '忽有数鸟东还' means 'suddenly several birds return east', '因与俱飞' means 'so he flies with them', and '疾乃倍于鸟' means 'the speed is twice that of the birds', indicating a sudden change in direction and speed.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 元邱校尉 - A title referring to a military officer, possibly a historical figure or a fictional character.
  • 佳人 - Refers to beautiful women, often used in classical literature to describe idealized female figures.
  • 斗艳歌 - Refers to a competition in singing, often associated with courtly or festive settings.
  • 斗柄 - Refers to the handle of the Big Dipper, a constellation used in Chinese astronomy.
  • 栏干 - Refers to railings, often used to describe a boundary or limit.
  • 天欲曙 - Refers to dawn, the time when the sky begins to lighten.
  • 华表 - Refers to ornamental pillars, often placed at the entrance of palaces or temples.
  • 双娥 - Refers to two beautiful women, often used in classical poetry to describe grace and beauty.
  • 褚先生 - A name, possibly referring to a historical figure or a fictional character.
  • 东城君 - A title or name, possibly referring to a historical figure or a fictional character.
  • 卜疑轩 - A place name, possibly referring to a pavilion or a building.
  • 狐 - Refers to foxes, often associated with trickery and supernatural abilities in Chinese folklore.
  • 隐语 - Refers to hidden or coded language, often used in classical literature to convey secret messages.
  • 狼狈 - Refers to a state of distress or confusion.
  • 舅所 - Refers to the uncle's place.

[207]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 少选 - Temporal expression meaning 'in a short while' or 'soon after.'
  • 见己身僵卧室中 - Vivid description of the protagonist finding himself stiff in bed, likely due to death.
  • 飞入者又一身也 - Metaphorical expression suggesting that the protagonist sees another version of himself, possibly a ghost or spirit.
  • 妻、子环卧身而哭 - Describes the wife and children surrounding the protagonist in bed, crying.
  • 己从旁慰止之 - The protagonist tries to comfort them from the side.
  • 妻、子略不顾 - The wife and children barely pay attention to the protagonist's attempts to comfort them.
  • 始悟己为异物 - The protagonist realizes he is an unusual being, possibly a ghost.
  • 犹忆气从口出 - The protagonist recalls breathing, a sign of life.
  • 因以首触口，试入焉 - The protagonist tries to breathe through his head, testing if he can return to life.
  • 豁然两身合为一体 - The protagonist and the other version of himself merge into one, suggesting a return to life.
  • 弥觉滞重 - The protagonist feels increasingly heavy and sluggish, contrasting with his previous lightness.
  • 不复如向者之翱翔自得矣 - The protagonist no longer feels the same freedom and self-satisfaction as before.
  • 尝夜行野外 - He once walked alone in the countryside at night.
  • 田叱之曰 - Tian Yi shouted at the ghost.
  • 尔何物 - "What are you?"
  • 鬼答言 - The ghost replied.
  • 我是鬼，尔是何物 - "I am a ghost. What are you?"
  • 田欲观其变 - Tian Yi wanted to see how the ghost would react.
  • 因绐之曰 - He then lied to the ghost.
  • 我亦鬼也 - "I am also a ghost."
  • 鬼大喜跃 - The ghost was very happy and jumped.
  • 遂来相嬲抱 - The ghost came to playfully embrace Tian Yi.
  • 体冷如冰 - The ghost's body was as cold as ice.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 庄生‘决疣溃痈’之说 - Refers to the Daoist philosopher Zhuangzi's metaphor of removing a pimple and draining an abscess, which suggests a process of purification or transformation.
  • 田卖鬼 - The nickname 'Tian the Ghost-Seller' reflects a cultural practice where people might engage in supernatural activities for profit.

Proper Nouns

  • 庄生 - Zhuangzi, a famous Daoist philosopher.
  • 田乙 - Tian Yi, a man who sells ghosts.

[208]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 鬼驚疑曰 - The ghost was startled and suspicious.
  • 公體太暖，恐非鬼 - Your body is too warm, perhaps you are not a ghost.
  • 我鬼中之壯盛者耳 - I am just a strong and vigorous ghost.
  • 善戲，願呈薄技 - I am good at performing tricks and would like to show you my modest skills.
  • 乃取頭顱著於腹，復著於尻，巳復著於胯，悉如生就，無少裂拆 - He then took his head and placed it on his stomach, then on his buttocks, and then on his hips, all as if it were a living person, without any tearing or splitting.
  • 或取頭分而二之，或三四之，或五六之，以至于十數，不等 - Sometimes he would split his head into two, sometimes into three or four, sometimes into five or six, up to ten, in various numbers.
  • 擲之空，投之水，旋轉之於地，已而復置之於項 - He would throw it into the air, drop it into the water, spin it on the ground, and then place it back on his neck.
  • 既復求田作戲 - He then asked me to perform a trick.
  • 田復绐之曰 - I replied to him in a deceitful manner.
  • 我饑甚，不暇作戲，將覓尋紹興市，爾能從乎 - I am very hungry and have no time to perform a trick. I am going to look for the city of Shaoxing. Can you accompany me?
  • 鬼欣然願偕往，彳亍而行 - The ghost happily agreed to go with me and we walked slowly together.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 紹興市 - Refers to the city of Shaoxing, a historical city in Zhejiang Province, known for its cultural heritage and wine.
  • 無常所 - No fixed place; refers to the transient nature of ghosts, who do not have a permanent dwelling.
  • 男子鼻涕 - Men's snot; a traditional belief that ghosts fear snot, possibly due to its association with life force and purity.

Proper Nouns

  • 紹興市 - The city of Shaoxing.
  • 男子鼻涕 - Men's snot.

[209]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 方行間 - Means 'while walking' or 'during the journey.'
  • 癯而長 - Describes a lean and tall appearance, possibly referring to a gaunt or emaciated figure.
  • 貌類枯木 - Describes an appearance similar to a withered tree, suggesting a withered or dead look.
  • 阿兄無恙 - A polite greeting meaning 'Is my elder brother well?'
  • 此亦我輩也 - Means 'This is also one of us,' indicating a sense of belonging or recognition.
  • 近通款洽焉 - Means 'to approach and engage in friendly conversation.'
  • 惧行 - A term that is ambiguous but likely means 'to walk together' or 'to proceed together.'
  • 天欲曉 - Means 'the sky is about to dawn' or 'it is getting light.'
  • 二鬼行漸緩 - Means 'the two ghosts' pace gradually slows down.'
  • 田恐其隱遁 - Means 'Tian fears they might disappear.'
  • 持愈急 - Means 'holds them more tightly.'
  • 化為兩鴨矣 - Means 'they transform into two ducks.'
  • 非非子曰 - The author's name, possibly a pseudonym or a reference to a specific individual.
  • 機智之能賣人者 - Refers to people who use their intelligence to deceive others.
  • 烏能及人之诡哉 - Means 'how could it compare to the deceit of humans?'
  • 真鬼當何如 - Means 'What about true ghosts?'
  • 世更有賣鬼之人也 - Means 'There are also people in the world who sell ghosts.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 田 - The name of the protagonist, possibly a common name.
  • 鬼 - Refers to ghosts or spirits, often associated with supernatural beings in Chinese folklore.
  • 市 - Refers to the market, a place where goods are bought and sold.
  • 羊豕 - Refers to sheep and pigs, common livestock in ancient China.
  • 魚鳥 - Refers to fish and birds, often used in metaphors or as symbols in Chinese culture.
  • 非非子 - The author's name, possibly a pseudonym or a reference to a specific individual. The term '非非' (feifei) can mean 'unconventional' or 'unusual.'

Proper Nouns

  • 田 - The name of the protagonist.
  • 非非子 - The author's name, possibly a pseudonym or a reference to a specific individual.

[210]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 陶生训蒙本里 - Literally means 'Taosheng teaches children in the village.' Sets the scene for the narrative, indicating that the protagonist, Taosheng, is a teacher in a rural setting.
  • 每夜自塾中归宿 - Means 'every night returns home from the school.' Describes Taosheng's routine.
  • 使婢预启关以待 - Means 'has the maid open the door in advance to wait.' Indicates that the wife prepares for Taosheng's return.
  • 审声而后纳之入 - Means 'after hearing his voice, she lets him in.' Shows the wife's cautiousness.
  • 若有不怿之色 - Means 'with an expression of displeasure.' Indicates that the wife is unhappy about something.
  • 殆非郎君也，何其异乎 - Means 'it's not you, how strange it is.' Expresses the wife's surprise at Taosheng's appearance.
  • 必有损德事 - Means 'there must be something that harms one's virtue.' Implies that Taosheng has done something wrong.
  • 吾过矣 - Means 'I have made a mistake.' Shows Taosheng's realization of his wrongdoing.
  • 邻人之嫂今将嫁 - Means 'my neighbor's sister-in-law is now getting married.' Provides context for the specific sin.
  • 鬼神怒我，其是故乎 - Means 'the spirits are angry with me, isn't this the reason?' Reflects Taosheng's fear and acknowledgment of his mistake.
  • 过莫大于破人节 - Means 'no fault is greater than breaking someone's chastity.' Emphasizes the gravity of the sin.
  • 而文书为凭 - Means 'and the document serves as proof.' Highlights the seriousness of the written contract.
  • 君不熟筹 - Means 'you did not think carefully.' Criticizes Taosheng's lack of foresight.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 塾中 - Refers to a private school or a small educational institution, often run by a single teacher.
  • 红纱灯笼 - Red silk lanterns, which were commonly used in traditional Chinese settings, symbolizing good fortune and prosperity.
  • 囊火 - Carrying a lamp, suggesting that Taosheng sometimes carried a light source when returning home.
  • 步月 - Walking under the moonlight, indicating that Taosheng sometimes walked back home at night.
  • 暗中彳亍 - Walking slowly and hesitantly in the dark, suggesting that Taosheng sometimes returned home in the dark.
  • 文书为凭 - Documents serve as evidence or proof, emphasizing the importance of written records in traditional Chinese society.

Proper Nouns

  • 陶生 - Taosheng, the protagonist of the story.
  • 邻人之嫂 - The neighbor's sister-in-law, who is getting married.
  • 红纱灯笼 - Red silk lanterns, a specific object in the story.

[211]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 肩此過矣 - Classical expression meaning 'bear this guilt' or 'take responsibility for this fault'; suggests a sense of moral burden or wrongdoing.
  • 若猶未行，尚可餌也 - If it has not yet been carried out, it may still be altered; the term 餌 (to entice or bait) is used metaphorically here, likely meaning 'to change' or 'to alter.'
  • 造鄰人之室 - Go to the neighbor’s house; a formal way of expressing visiting someone’s home.
  • 婚帖去否 - Has the marriage notice been sent? 婚帖 is a formal marriage notice or engagement document.
  • 其期在明日 - The date is tomorrow; a straightforward statement of a future event.
  • 宰甚幸甚 - A formal and exaggerated expression of happiness or relief; literally 'The magistrate is very happy indeed.' 宰 refers to a local official or magistrate.
  • 當改作 - It should be changed; a formal way of requesting an alteration.
  • 公嫁嫂已不義 - You are marrying your sister-in-law, which is unjust; a strong moral condemnation of the act.
  • 吾豈助公為不義者乎 - How could I help you commit an injustice? A rhetorical question expressing moral refusal.
  • 事不諧 - The matter did not come to fruition; it means the marriage did not proceed.
  • 紅燈之見如初矣 - The red lamp was seen as before; suggests a supernatural or symbolic return to the previous state.
  • 仕宦 - To serve in official posts; refers to a career in government.
  • 歷清顯之職 - Held positions of clear and honorable rank; indicates a successful and respected career.
  • 非非子曰 - A classical style of commentary; likely the name of the commentator or author, similar to 'Sikuangzi' or 'Zhuangzi.'
  • 高明之家，鬼瞰其室 - A classical allusion to the idea that the virtuous are watched by spirits, and the unvirtuous are under scrutiny; comes from the *Book of Documents* or similar classical texts.

  • 怀抱乳哺，三岁而夭折 - 怀抱乳哺: was held and fed; 三岁: three years old; 而夭折: and died young. He was held and fed, but died young at three years old.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 府试 - Local examination, a part of the imperial examination system in ancient China.
  • 魑魅 - Evil spirits or demons, often used in literature to describe supernatural entities.
  • 馆所 - Inn or lodging place, commonly used for travelers.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 迷路，至一山，不辨何地 - This sequence must be read as a series of events: being lost, arriving at a mountain, and not recognizing the location.
  • 荆棘四塞，溪涧环之 - This phrase describes the environment as being surrounded by thorns and brambles and streams and ravines.
  • 遂共坐树下待旦 - This clause describes the action of sitting under a tree and waiting until dawn.
  • 及明观之，乃其中一人之村后山也 - This clause explains the realization of the location.
  • 甚平坦而无所阻 - This phrase describes the environment as very flat with no obstacles.
  • 知迷魑魅矣 - This clause indicates the realization of being lost in the realm of evil spirits.
  • 分道而归 - This phrase describes the action of separating and returning home.
  • 而李亦旋至馆所 - This clause describes Li's arrival at the inn.
  • 觉神情恍惚，卧起不舒 - This phrase describes Li's mental state and physical discomfort.
  • 忽思归宿，而去家稍远 - This clause describes Li's sudden desire to return home and the distance from home.
  • 主人止之 - This clause describes the host stopping Li.
  • 殊勿听，遽袖火以行 - This clause describes Li's determination to go despite the host's advice.
  • 天晓不能至 - This clause describes Li's failure to reach his destination when it became light.
  • 过一村郭，旧游处也 - This clause describes passing a village, a place he had visited before.
  • 茫然似初经也者 - This phrase describes looking blankly as if seeing it for the first time.
  • 薄暮始至家 - This clause describes finally arriving home in the late afternoon.
  • 程不二十里，凡行一夜及一日 - This phrase describes the distance and the duration of travel.
  • 见其妻，若不识，问曰：‘谁欤?’ - This clause describes seeing his wife as if not recognizing her and asking, 'Who are you?'
  • 妻笑曰：‘李某之妻也。何目谬至此！’ - This clause describes his wife's response, laughing and saying, 'I am Li's wife. How could you mistake me?'
  • 既同寝好合，乃豁然而悟 - This clause describes the realization after sleeping together.
  • 谓我数日行止乃如梦中也，爽然自失者良久 - This phrase describes the realization that his actions over several days were like a dream, and he was greatly disappointed and lost for a long time.
  • 是夜妻有娠，明年举一子 - This phrase describes his wife becoming pregnant that night and giving birth to a son the next year.
  • 体貌怪异，不甚类人 - This phrase describes the son having a strange appearance, not very human-like.
  • 亦不啼，亦不笑 - This phrase describes the son not crying or smiling.
  • 怀抱乳哺，三岁而夭折 - This phrase describes the son being held and fed but dying young at three years old.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 蓋魅之將托生其家也 - Classical construction meaning 'when a ghost intends to reincarnate in their family,' used to introduce the narrative.
  • 故迷之於路，迫之以歸也 - Means 'therefore, they lead them astray on the road and force them to return home,' a metaphor for the ghost's manipulation.
  • 嗟乎! - An interjection expressing sorrow or lamentation.
  • 將欲父之，而先魅之 - Means 'intending to father them, but first bewitching them,' a critique of the ghost's deceitful actions.
  • 鬼之無賴亦甚矣 - Means 'the ghost's recklessness is indeed great,' a judgment on the ghost's behavior.
  • 靦然者一鬼，亦蠢然者一人 - Means 'one shameless ghost, and one equally foolish human,' a parallel construction highlighting the mutual folly.
  • 又曷怪焉 - Means 'how could one not be surprised,' a rhetorical question emphasizing the absurdity of the situation.
  • 下帷攻苦 - Classical idiom meaning 'studying diligently under a curtain,' indicating intense focus on study.
  • 罕與外交 - Means 'rarely interacts with others,' describing the scholar's isolation.
  • 心愛之 - Means 'loves it in his heart,' expressing the scholar's affection.
  • 日暮低徊焉 - Means 'lingers at dusk,' describing the scholar's behavior.
  • 谷見惑之 - Means 'is bewitched by her,' indicating the scholar's infatuation.
  • 遂有玉洞桃花之約 - Means 'thus they have a secret rendezvous like a jade cave and peach blossom,' a metaphor for a romantic encounter.
  • 西行至一山 - Means 'travels west to a mountain,' setting the scene.
  • 危峰連亘 - Means 'dangerous peaks stretching endlessly,' describing the mountain's appearance.
  • 奇峭插雲 - Means 'strange and steep, piercing the clouds,' further describing the mountain's grandeur.
  • 時已昏暮 - Means 'it is already dusk,' indicating the time of day.
  • 谷惶遽不行 - Means 'the scholar is too frightened to move,' describing his fear.
  • 女子故绐曰 - Means 'the woman intentionally deceives him,' indicating her deceit.
  • 山君且至 - Means 'the mountain god is coming,' a reference to a mythical being.
  • 谷驚而仆 - Means 'the scholar is startled and falls down,' describing his reaction.
  • 女子以手挥之曰 - Means 'the woman waves her hand and says,' indicating her action and speech.
  • 去！毋惊我郎也 - Means 'Go away! Don't scare my husband,' a command to the tiger.
  • 又一虎继至 - Means 'another tiger follows,' describing the second tiger's appearance.
  • 眈眈视谷 - Means 'stares at the scholar,' describing the tiger's gaze.
  • 谷神情慌乱 - Means 'the scholar is in a state of panic,' describing his emotional state.
  • 请返者再 - Means 'asks to return twice,' indicating his repeated requests.
  • 女子以袖拂谷面 - Means 'the woman wipes the scholar's face with her sleeve,' describing her action.
  • 调之曰 - Means 'teases him and says,' indicating her playful tone.
  • 年少书生，怯弱乃尔 - Means 'a young scholar, so timid,' a comment on the scholar's character.
  • 今已至此，隔此一岭耳 - Means 'now that we are here, there is only one mountain ridge left,' a reassurance to the scholar.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 魅 - Refers to a ghost or spirit, often associated with supernatural manipulation.
  • 芙蓉館掃花女 - Likely a fictional character, possibly a courtesan or a supernatural being in a story.
  • 山君 - Refers to a mountain god or a powerful spirit associated with the mountains.
  • 玉洞桃花 - A metaphor for a secluded and beautiful place, often associated with romantic or idyllic settings in classical literature.

Proper Nouns

  • 谷 - The name of the scholar.
  • 山君 - A mythical being associated with the mountains.
  • 玉洞桃花 - A metaphorical place name, likely referring to a secluded and beautiful location.

  • 暴风大作，舟覆中流 - '暴风' means 'strong wind,' '大作' means 'blow strongly,' '舟覆' means 'the boat capsized,' and '中流' means 'in the middle of the stream.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 前行數武 - Literal phrase meaning 'to advance several steps,' used to describe a short distance.
  • 崔嵬千仞 - Describes a towering, rugged mountain peak, literally 'thousands of feet high.'
  • 壁立如削 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'standing like a wall, as if cut by a knife,' describing a sheer cliff.
  • 仰視股栗 - Means 'to look up and tremble with fear,' a common expression for feeling intimidated or scared.
  • 疑巨鬼欲來扑人 - Hyperbolic expression meaning 'to suspect that a giant ghost is about to attack,' emphasizing the fear and awe of the scene.
  • 不覺變色，抚膺長嘆 - Describes a reaction of surprise and deep sigh, literally 'unconsciously changing color and sighing deeply.'
  • 郎乃畏勞乎? - Rhetorical question meaning 'Are you afraid of hard work?' used to express disbelief or challenge.
  • 不歷險阻艱難而成好事古，蓋亦寡矣 - Complex sentence meaning 'In ancient times, it was rare to achieve good things without going through dangers and difficulties.'
  • 既畏之，當另觅坦途，无怠厥志也 - Means 'If you fear it, find another easy path, but do not slacken your resolve.'
  • 抽簪於石壁畫之 - Describes the act of drawing a hairpin on a stone wall, a metaphorical action.
  • 石随簪而中辟 - Means 'the stone splits open along the line drawn by the hairpin.'
  • 望如深巷，幽暗不见人 - Describes the appearance of the split stone, literally 'looking like a deep alley, dark and empty.'
  • 曳谷而行，略无踬碍 - Means 'dragging Gu along, with no obstacles.'
  • 约二里许 - Rough estimate of distance, meaning 'about two li.'
  • 忽然开旷 - Describes a sudden change in the landscape, literally 'suddenly open and spacious.'
  • 掩息幽房，悄语相对 - Means 'to enter a quiet room, whispering to each other.'
  • 出酒果款谷 - Describes the act of offering wine and fruit to Gu, literally 'offering wine and fruit to Gu.'
  • 情态甚殷 - Means 'with great enthusiasm or sincerity.'
  • 谈道有年 - Means 'to have talked about the Dao for many years.'
  • 奈何效长卿薄行? - Rhetorical question meaning 'Why should I imitate Zixu's light behavior?' referring to Sima Xiangru's reputation for licentiousness.
  • 宜向皋比座前去演也 - Means 'you should go to the seat of the tiger skin to perform,' a metaphorical reference to engaging in scholarly or philosophical discourse.
  • 衾枕焕烂，兰麝熏人 - Describes the luxurious and fragrant bedding, literally 'the bed and pillows are splendid, and the scent of orchid and musk permeates the air.'

  • 剑 - Refers to the sword, a symbol of power and protection in Chinese culture.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 他日归，因重造妇室 - On another day, returning, he rebuilt the wife's room or The next day when he returned, he rebuilt the wife's room.
  • 妇见生，以为搴裳来就也，急引入内寝 - The wife saw the guest and thought he was coming to her, so she quickly led him into the inner chamber.
  • 具茗饮已，复设酒馔 - After preparing tea and drinks, she then set up wine and food.
  • 生愕然致谢，谓主人款客何太厚，欲辞去 - The guest was startled and thanked the host, saying that the hospitality was too much, and wanted to leave.
  • 妇坚留之，而挑以微词，生亦不解 - The wife strongly kept him and flirted with subtle remarks, but the guest did not understand.
  • 妇复整鬟易服，为华艳之妆 - The wife then neatened her hair and changed her clothes, putting on elegant and beautiful makeup.
  • 以手抓生面，而摩其痕曰 - She grabbed the face of the guest and rubbed the mark, saying, 'Young master, your face is as red as pomegranate and peach blossoms.'
  • 尔忍杀我，即使杀，终不教尔去 - Are you willing to kill me? Even if you kill me, I will never let you go.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 洎暮夫归 - By evening when the husband returned. '洎' indicates a temporal marker, suggesting that the action occurred by a specific time.
  • 见而骇恸 - He saw and was greatly shocked and wept. '骇恸' is a compound expression meaning extreme shock and grief.
  • 有邻父者，妇之假父也 - There was a neighbor who was the woman's stepfather. '假父' refers to a stepfather or adoptive father.
  • 尝出入其家 - Who often came and went from their house. This implies frequent visits.
  • 大疑之 - He greatly suspected him. '大疑' means to strongly suspect or doubt.
  • 执而讼之官 - He seized him and took him to court. '讼之官' means to take someone to court.
  • 迫于刑，遂诬服 - Under pressure from torture, he confessed falsely. '迫于刑' means to be forced by torture, and '诬服' means to falsely confess.
  • 狱具矣 - The case was settled. '狱具' means the case is complete or settled.
  • 急诣县自陈 - He hurriedly went to the county to explain himself. '诣' means to go to, and '自陈' means to explain oneself.
  • 具述本末 - He fully recounted the beginning and end. '本末' refers to the beginning and end or the whole story.
  • 愿为死囚偿妇命 - He was willing to take the place of the dead prisoner to save his wife's life. '偿妇命' means to take the place of the wife's life.
  • 夫闻之，慨然曰 - The husband heard and said with a sigh. '慨然' means with a sigh or with a sense of resignation.
  • 有妇若此，宜死久矣 - To have a wife like this, he should have died long ago. '宜死久矣' means he should have died a long time ago.
  • 感君盛德，为我杀之 - Moved by your great virtue, kill him for me. '盛德' means great virtue.
  • 不然，其辱我滋甚 - Otherwise, the shame I would suffer would be even greater. '滋甚' means even greater.
  • 今何敢复相累乎 - Now how dare I burden you again? '相累' means to burden someone.
  • 愿息讼以归 - He wished to end the lawsuit and return home. '息讼' means to end the lawsuit.
  • 县宰义之，允其请，而加礼于生 - The county magistrate found him righteous and granted his request, treating him with respect. '义之' means found him righteous, and '加礼于生' means treated him with respect.
  • 非非子曰 - The Non-Non Son said. '非非子' is a pen name or pseudonym.
  • 生真伟丈夫乎哉 - Is this man truly a great man? '伟丈夫' means a great man.
  • 其事，鲁男子之所不及，而柳季之所难也 - His actions surpass those of the man from Lu and are difficult for Liu Ji to match. '鲁男子' refers to a man from the state of Lu, and '柳季' refers to Liu Ji, a historical figure known for his integrity.
  • 然既已受美人之贻，入之子之室 - However, since he has already received the gift from the beautiful woman and entered her house. '美人之贻' means a gift from a beautiful woman.
  • 与偷香钻穴者何以异 - What is different from those who secretly steal fragrances and burrow through walls? '偷香钻穴' refers to secret romantic encounters.
  • 则慎始之道已失 - Then the principle of caution at the beginning has been lost. '慎始' means caution at the beginning.
  • 及其抱璧将投、按剑相盼 - When he held the jade and was about to throw it, and pressed his sword and looked at each other. '抱璧将投' means holding the jade and about to throw it, and '按剑相盼' means pressing the sword and looking at each other.
  • 亦何难翻然高逝、与彼长辞 - Why couldn't he suddenly fly away and bid farewell to them forever? '翻然高逝' means to suddenly fly away, and '长辞' means to bid farewell forever.
  • 完其节而复全其躯 - Preserve his integrity and complete his body. '完其节' means to preserve his integrity, and '复全其躯' means to complete his body.
  • 斯盛德之至也 - This is the highest form of great virtue. '盛德之至' means the highest form of great virtue.
  • 而必加刃焉 - But he insisted on being killed. '加刃' means to be killed.
  • 君子以为忍矣 - The gentlemen considered it cruel. '忍矣' means cruel.
  • 论法；杀人者死 - According to the law, the killer must die. '论法' means according to the law.
  • 即原情末减，亦自有应得之罪 - Even if mitigated by circumstances, there is still a deserved punishment. '原情末减' means mitigated by circumstances, and '应得之罪' means deserved punishment.
  • 其得脱然无累也，幸矣 - That he could escape without burden is fortunate. '脱然无累' means to escape without burden.
  • 至观其毅然自投，无少违避 - To see him resolutely throw himself into it, without any avoidance. '毅然自投' means to resolutely throw himself, and '无少违避' means without any avoidance.
  • 则不谓之伟丈夫不可也 - Then it cannot be denied that he is a great man. '不谓之伟丈夫不可也' means it cannot be denied that he is a great man.

  • 瓜李生嫌 - Metaphor for avoiding suspicion, derived from the story of a man and a woman who were suspected of having an affair.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '乃婉姑以碧玉破瓜之年，抱绿珠捐粉之恨' is a compound structure where '乃婉姑' is the subject, and '以碧玉破瓜之年' and '抱绿珠捐粉之恨' are descriptive phrases.
  • The sentence '人皆惜玉，畴弗伤心?' is a rhetorical question, where '人皆惜玉' is the main clause, and '畴弗伤心?' is the question.
  • The sentence '我不偷香，亦为茹叹！' is a simple sentence with a subject '我' and a predicate '不偷香，亦为茹叹！'.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 城东有小宅一区 - Refers to a small house located in the eastern part of a city; in classical Chinese, '城东' (east of the city) is a common way to describe a location.

  • 纸窗石炕 - Paper windows and stone beds were common in the homes of the lower or middle classes in ancient China, especially in northern regions; paper windows were practical but not as insulating as wood or bamboo.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 秋气侵人骨 - Vivid expression meaning 'the autumn air penetrates one's bones,' evoking a deep chill that affects the body and soul.
  • 栖鹘叫层崖 - Describes a perched falcon (or eagle) calling out from a layered cliff, creating a haunting and solitary atmosphere.
  • 乱星堕林莽 - Means 'stars falling into the dense forest,' a poetic way of describing the scattering of stars in the night sky, possibly alluding to the chaos and unpredictability of nature.
  • 无入夜转深 - Means 'as night deepens, there is no one around,' emphasizing the isolation and stillness of the night.
  • 岩洞孤泉响 - Describes the sound of a lone spring echoing in a rocky cave, adding to the eerie and isolated setting.

  • 争献山果 - Means 'competing to offer mountain fruits,' showing the monkeys' hospitality.
  • 饱啖而反 - Means 'eating their fill and returning,' indicating a satisfying experience.
  • 乾糇告匮 - Means 'our provisions ran out,' indicating the end of the journey.
  • 恸哭送余 - Means 'crying bitterly to see me off,' expressing the ghost's deep emotional attachment.
  • 逾年复往 - Means 'more than a year later, I returned,' indicating a significant passage of time.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 道士隱其姓，名希客 - Classical structure where the surname is omitted ('隱其姓') and only the given name is provided; common in Classical Chinese to imply anonymity or focus on the individual's name.
  • 女子姓阮，名西娘 - Standard formula for naming a woman: surname first, then given name.
  • 驿亭之鬼，姓练名嵌 - Similar structure to the above, but applied to a ghost ('鬼'), indicating supernatural beings are given surnames and names in this text.
  • 逆旅少年 - Compound noun meaning 'a young man at an inn' or 'a young traveler at an inn.'
  • 道遇骑使 - '道遇' means 'met on the road' or 'encountered on the way'; '骑使' refers to a mounted messenger or official on horseback.
  • 一一亦往制府勾当公事者 - Complex clause meaning 'who were also going to the provincial administration to handle official business.' The structure is elliptical and must be expanded in translation.
  • 因同行 - Simple conjunction meaning 'therefore they traveled together.'
  • 解装逆旅 - '解装' means to unpack or remove one's luggage; '逆旅' is an inn or lodging house. So this phrase means 'unpacked at the inn.'
  • 北寝已有宿客 - '北寝' refers to the northern chamber or room; '宿客' is a guest staying overnight. So this means 'the northern room already had a guest staying.'
  • 遂止东偏 - '遂' means 'then' or 'thus'; '止' is to stop or stay; '东偏' means the eastern side or corner. So this phrase means 'so he stayed in the eastern corner.'
  • 顷见少年自外入 - '顷' means 'shortly after' or 'soon'; '见' is 'saw'; '自外入' means 'entered from outside.' So this is 'soon after, he saw a young man entering from outside.'
  • 深笠宽衣 - '深笠' is a deep hat or hood; '宽衣' is loose clothing. This describes the young man's appearance.
  • 面掩于笠，不甚可辨 - '面掩于笠' means 'his face was hidden under the hat'; '不甚可辨' means 'not very distinguishable.' So this is 'his face was hidden under the hat, making him not very recognizable.'
  • 即北寝客也 - '即' means 'was'; '北寝客' is 'the guest in the northern room.' So this is 'he was the guest in the northern room.'
  • 趣召主人，使治具待客，必丰必洁 - '趣召' is 'hurriedly summoned'; '使治具待客' means 'to have the host prepare food and wait on the guest'; '必丰必洁' is 'must be abundant and clean.' So this is 'he hurriedly summoned the host to prepare food and wait on the guest, making sure it was abundant and clean.'
  • 主人诺，去 - '诺' is an affirmation or agreement; '去' is 'left.' So this is 'the host agreed and left.'
  • 曲肱支颐，凝灯宴坐 - '曲肱' is to bend one’s arm; '支颐' is to support one’s chin (a posture of sitting); '凝灯' is to gaze at the lamp; '宴坐' is to sit in leisure. So this is 'he bent his arm, supported his chin, and sat leisurely by the lamp.'
  • 陈亦遂就榻 - '陈' is the name of the character; '亦遂' is 'also then'; '就榻' is 'went to the bed.' So this is 'Chen also then went to his bed.'
  • 骑使素好选事，秣马储刍已 - '素好选事' means 'has always liked to choose matters' or 'has always been fond of selecting tasks'; '秣马储刍' means 'fed the horse and stored fodder.' So this is 'the mounted messenger, who had always liked to choose matters, had already fed the horse and stored fodder.'
  • 潜往窥之，伏于梁间 - '潜往' is 'secretly went'; '窥之' is 'to look at him'; '伏于梁间' is 'hid under the beam.' So this is 'he secretly went to look at him, hiding under the beam.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 道士 - 'Daoist' or 'monk' from the Daoist tradition. In this context, it is a nameless Daoist (隐其姓).
  • 希客 - A Daoist’s given name, possibly symbolic or metaphorical.
  • 驿亭 - A驿站 (yàzhàn), a waystation or post station on the road, used for travelers and official messengers.
  • 鬼 - Ghost or spirit, commonly encountered in Chinese folklore and literature.
  • 逆旅 - A term for an inn or lodging house, often used in Classical Chinese literature.
  • 制府 - A provincial administration or governor’s office, likely referring to a military or administrative center.
  • 骑使 - A mounted official or messenger, common in imperial administration.
  • 北寝 - The northern room, indicating a division of space in an inn or house.
  • 东偏 - The eastern corner or side, a less desirable room in a traditional Chinese layout.
  • 曲肱支颐 - A posture of leisure or meditation, referencing Confucian or Daoist ideals of simplicity.
  • 凝灯宴坐 - A poetic description of leisurely sitting by a lamp, possibly with a meditative or contemplative air.
  • 梁间 - Under a beam, a common hiding place in traditional Chinese architecture.

Proper Nouns

  • 陈孝靡奇圣 - A person’s name; possibly a fictional or symbolic character. The name may be a composite or poetic name. Transliterated as Chen Xiaomí Qíshèng.
  • 京师 - The capital city, likely referring to the imperial capital (e.g., Chang’an or later Nanjing).
  • 金陵 - A historical name for Nanjing, the capital of the Southern Song dynasty and later the Ming dynasty.
  • 制府 - A term for a provincial administration or governor’s office; may be rendered as provincial administration or governor’s office.
  • 骑使 - A mounted messenger or official; may be rendered as mounted messenger or riding official.
  • 逆旅 - A term for an inn or lodging house; may be rendered as inn or traveling inn.
  • 北寝 - The northern room or chamber; may be rendered as northern room.
  • 东偏 - The eastern side or corner; may be rendered as eastern corner.
  • 梁间 - Under a beam; may be rendered as under the beam.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 疾驅而行 - Compound verb phrase meaning 'to drive quickly' or 'to rush forward'; *疾* (quickly) modifies *驅* (to drive or urge forward), suggesting haste or urgency.
  • 抵一处，嘈杂类市镇 - *抵* means 'to arrive at'; *嘈杂* means 'noisy' or 'crowded'; *类市镇* is a simile meaning 'like a market town.' This phrase sets the scene of a bustling place.
  • 西有崇台，优伶演剧其上，其下列席焉 - *崇台* is a high platform or stage; *优伶* refers to actors or performers (*优* as entertainers, *伶* as musicians or actors); *演剧* means 'to perform a play'; *列席* means 'to sit in rows' or 'to be seated in a line.'
  • 宾客甚盛 - *宾客* is 'guests' or 'visitors'; *甚盛* means 'very numerous' or 'great in number.'
  • 克与五人亦共据一席 - *据一席* is a metaphor for 'occupying a seat' or 'sharing a place'; *席* can also mean a table or a place to sit in a banquet setting.
  • 酒馔随至，亦不知主人谁也 - *酒馔* is a compound noun for 'wine and food'; *随至* means 'arrived promptly' or 'came along with'; *亦不知主人谁也* is a common Classical Chinese construction meaning 'also did not know who the host was.'
  • 克素不习酒 - *素* means 'usually' or 'originally'; *不习酒* means 'not accustomed to drinking alcohol.'
  • 抱病未瘳 - *抱病* means 'to suffer from an illness'; *未瘳* means 'not yet recovered' or 'not yet healed.'
  • 绝不敢饮澉 - *绝不敢* is a strong negation: 'not dare at all'; *饮澉* is likely a misspelling or archaic term. A plausible reading is *饮酣* (to drink to intoxication) or *饮甘* (to drink sweet wine). Given the context, *饮酣* may be the intended term, meaning 'to drink to the point of intoxication.' However, this is uncertain.
  • 饕餮弥甚，杯盘尽罄 - *饕餮* is a classical term for gluttony or excessive eating; *弥甚* means 'even more so'; *杯盘尽罄* means 'the cups and plates were all emptied.'
  • 少焉皆下，入一家 - *少焉* is a time adverb meaning 'after a while' or 'shortly'; *皆下* means 'all got off'; *入一家* means 'entered a house.'
  • 克之姨之家也 - *姨之家* is a classical expression meaning 'the home of one's aunt' or 'the family of one's aunt.'
  • 御者直拥至犬窝 - *直拥* is a compound verb meaning 'to push directly' or 'to carry straight'; *犬窝* is 'dog's den' or 'doghouse.'
  • 递推五人仆，皆成小犬 - *递推* is a sequential verb meaning 'to push one after another'; *仆* is a verb meaning 'to fall' or 'to be thrown down'; *皆成小犬* means 'all became small dogs.'
  • 克恐，欲走避 - *恐* is 'to fear'; *欲走避* is 'wanted to run away.'
  • 亦为所推，大呼昏去 - *亦为所推* is a passive construction meaning 'was also pushed'; *大呼昏去* is 'shouted loudly and fainted.'
  • 比苏，则身亦犬矣 - *比苏* is 'when he awoke'; *则身亦犬矣* is a conclusion: 'then his body was also a dog.'
  • 遍体痛楚，母犬为舔之，痛顿止 - *遍体痛楚* is 'all over his body was painful'; *母犬为舔之* is 'the mother dog licked him'; *痛顿止* is 'the pain suddenly stopped.'
  • 克呼姨，弗应 - *呼姨* is 'called out to his aunt'; *弗应* is 'did not respond.'
  • 遂入内 - *遂* is 'then' or 'therefore'; *入内* is 'entered the house.'
  • 群狗皆争食母乳 - *群狗* is 'the pack of dogs'; *争食* is 'competed to eat'; *母乳* is 'mother's milk.'
  • 克亦觉乳香，饥欲食 - *觉乳香* is 'felt the milk was fragrant'; *饥欲食* is 'was hungry and wanted to eat.'
  • 我人也，奈何食狗乳？ - *我人也* is 'I am a human'; *奈何* is 'how can I'; *食狗乳* is 'eat dog's milk.'
  • 欲趋归，足弱小能运 - *趋归* is 'to hurry back'; *足弱小能运* is 'his feet were too weak and small to move.'
  • 强行数步，母犬辄衔至故处 - *强行* is 'forced himself to walk'; *数步* is 'a few steps'; *母犬辄衔至故处* is 'the mother dog would carry him back to his original place.'
  • 中心凄然自伤，遂为异物也 - *中心凄然* is 'in his heart, he felt sorrow'; *自伤* is 'self-pity'; *遂为异物也* is 'thus became a different being.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 优伶 - Refers to performers or actors, particularly in the context of traditional Chinese theater. *优* originally meant 'entertainers,' and *伶* meant 'musicians' or 'performers.'
  • 御者 - The driver or coachman, a common figure in classical Chinese texts describing journeys or transportation.
  • 姨之家 - 'The family of one's aunt' or 'the aunt’s house.' In classical Chinese, *姨* can mean 'aunt' or 'uncle’s wife,' depending on context.
  • 犬窝 - A dog's den or kennel, a common term for where dogs live.
  • 母犬 - The mother dog, used to refer to the female parent of the puppies.
  • 犬产六子矣 - The dog has given birth to six puppies. This is a literal and metaphorical statement, as the protagonist is now a dog.
  • 群狗皆争食母乳 - A scene of puppies competing for their mother's milk, a natural and common occurrence in animal life.
  • 异物 - A term used to describe something strange or otherworldly, often used in classical Chinese to describe transformation or a being that is not human.

  • 异哉所闻 - Means 'strange is what I have heard,' indicating that the author finds the account unusual or surprising.
  • 故采入稗说 - Means 'therefore, I have included it in trivial sayings,' indicating that the author has chosen to record the account despite its questionable nature.
  • 未足以为信也 - Means 'it is not sufficient to be taken as true,' indicating that the author does not consider the account reliable.
  • 要其负才藏用 - Means 'in summary, he was talented but kept his abilities hidden,' indicating that the person in question was capable but did not openly display his talents.

  • 病绝数四 - Means 'she was on the verge of death several times,' indicating that the concubine was gravely ill.
  • 怀小刀，将自为计 - Means 'she carried a small knife, intending to take her own life,' indicating that the concubine was considering suicide.
  • 家人见而夺之 - Means 'her family saw and took the knife from her,' indicating that her family intervened to prevent her from committing suicide.
  • 慷慨自矢，坚不欲生 - Means 'she was resolute and determined not to live,' indicating that the concubine was firm in her decision to die.
  • 或绐之曰 - Means 'someone deceived her by saying,' indicating that someone gave her false information to change her mind.

  • 七日将复焉 - Means 'in seven days, he will return,' indicating that the concubine was told that her husband would return after seven days.

  • 盖冀其日远哀杀而志可回也 - Means 'this was perhaps to delay her grief and change her mind,' indicating that the deception was intended to give the concubine time to reconsider her decision.
  • 姬许诺 - Means 'the concubine agreed,' indicating that the concubine accepted the deception.
  • 由是晏然食息 - Means 'from then on, she ate and slept calmly,' indicating that the concubine became peaceful and calm.

  • 信乎？ - Is this true?; 信 is to believe or be true.
  • 虽然，何以处之？ - Although so, how should we deal with it?; 虽然 is although, 何以 is how to, 处之 is deal with it.
  • 君欲得女耶？得金耶？ - Do you want the woman or the money?; 欲 is to desire, 得女 is to obtain the woman, 得金 is to obtain money.
  • 能兼得甚幸，不然，则得女而甘心焉 - If you can obtain both, it would be very fortunate. Otherwise, obtaining the woman would be satisfactory; 兼得 is to obtain both, 甚幸 is very fortunate, 不然 is otherwise, 甘心 is to be content.
  • 讼之可也 - You can take it to court; 讼 is to litigate or take to court.
  • 讼其父，是仇之也 - To litigate against her father is to make an enemy of him; 仇之也 is to make him your enemy.
  • 女必不从，或有他变，可惜也 - The woman will certainly not comply, or there may be other complications, which would be a pity; 必不从 is certainly not comply, 或有他变 is or there may be other changes, 可惜也 is it would be a pity.
  • 欲得女，则戚也 - If you want the woman, then you become related by marriage; 戚 is a relative by marriage.
  • 顾诚居女为奇货，更挟以求公，公能应之乎？ - Gu truly treats his daughter as a rare treasure, and he would use her as leverage to demand something from you. Can you respond to that?; 奇货 is a rare or valuable item, 挟以求公 is to use her as leverage to demand something from you, 应之 is to respond.
  • 君意谓何？ - What is your meaning?; 意谓 is your intention or meaning.
  • 某请说之，明日当报命 - I will explain it to you; I will report back tomorrow; 某 is I, 请说之 is I will explain it, 报命 is to report back or give an account.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 闽上 - Refers to a region in the south of China, likely Fujian; in classical Chinese, 闽 is often used to refer to Fujian province.
  • 顾 - A surname, possibly referring to a specific historical figure or fictional character; no specific historical figure is identifiable from this name alone.
  • 隐树间，伺其出 - Reflects a traditional narrative technique in Chinese fiction, where characters hide and wait to observe others, often used in stories involving espionage, romance, or intrigue.
  • 讼之可也 - Litigation is a legal method in ancient China, often used to resolve disputes, especially in matters of inheritance, marriage, or property.
  • 戚也 - Refers to a familial or marital relationship, indicating that the man would be related to the woman through marriage if he takes her.
  • 奇货 - A term that evokes the idea of a rare or valuable item, possibly a metaphor for a daughter who is considered a 'treasure' by her family; it also echoes the famous story of Lü Buwei, who once said that a woman is a '奇货可居' (a rare item worth investing in).
  • 挟以求公 - This phrase suggests that the father is using his daughter as a bargaining chip or leverage to gain something from the man (possibly money, status, or a favor).
  • 报命 - This phrase is common in classical Chinese narrative, where a character promises to report back or fulfill a task at a later time, often used in dialogues between characters.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 念乃寝 - Means 'his thoughts then ceased' or 'his mind then rested,' suggesting a resolution or acceptance.
  • 家渐替 - Means 'the family gradually declined,' indicating a deterioration in family status or wealth.
  • 行亦少敛 - Means 'his behavior also became somewhat restrained,' suggesting a change in conduct.
  • 委禽焉 - Literally means 'to entrust a goose,' an idiom for arranging a marriage.

  • 羞窘无策 - Means 'ashamed and at a loss,' indicating a state of embarrassment and helplessness.

  • 绝姣丽 - Means 'extremely beautiful,' describing the concubine's appearance.

  • 顾亦非顾 - Means 'it is not even Con,' indicating a denial of identity.

  • 奸辈买妾以诱君 - Means 'the villains bought me to lure you,' indicating a plot against the protagonist.
  • 败叶随风，孤英落溷 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'fallen leaves follow the wind, and solitary flowers fall into the latrine,' symbolizing a decline in status and purity.
  • 已三易婿矣 - Means 'I have already changed husbands three times,' indicating a history of multiple marriages.

  • 久之，偶下山 - Means 'after a long time, I occasionally went down the mountain,' indicating a change in its behavior.
  • 闻金鼓声，其恐僳，遂不敢复出 - Means 'I heard the sound of drums and was terrified, so I dared not come out again,' describing its fear.

  • 携以出山，逾月而死 - Means 'they took it out of the mountain, and it died after a month,' describing its fate.

  • 硝 - Refers to nitre, a mineral used in various industrial and agricultural processes.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 有商於蜀耆言之 - Introduces a narrative where an elder from Shu is reporting something; structure similar to 'It was said by an elder from Shu.'
  • 壁虱 - Wall louse or mite; can also be used metaphorically for hidden dangers.
  • 某氏女子 - A woman from a certain family; '某' denotes an unspecified or anonymous person.
  • 夢黑甲人為祟 - Dreamed of a black-armored person causing a calamity; '祟' refers to a ghost or supernatural harm.
  • 其家患之 - Her family was troubled by it; '患' means to suffer or be troubled by something.
  • 自樓來 - It came from the upper floor; '樓' refers to a building or upper room.
  • 舁下焚殺之 - They carried it down and burned it to death; '舁' means to carry on a pole or stretcher; '焚殺' is to burn and kill.
  • 怪遂絕 - The supernatural occurrences ceased; '怪' refers to the supernatural or strange phenomena.
  • 日就赢尪 - Day by day he became thin and weak; '赢尪' is a compound word meaning emaciated or weak.
  • 夜燭之 - At night, they lit a lamp to look at it; '燭' can mean to light a lamp or to inspect with a lamp.
  • 伏甲胸 - Lying on the man’s chest; '伏' means to lie down or be in a crouched position.
  • 小者萬計 - There were tens of thousands of small ones; an exaggerated expression to emphasize the number.
  • 周身而集，無隙地 - They gathered all over his body, leaving no space; '無隙地' means 'no place without them.'
  • 見燈即引去 - Upon seeing the lamp, they withdrew; '引去' means to retreat or move away.
  • 入础旁穴中 - They entered the hole beside the base; '础' refers to the base or foundation of a building; '穴' is a hole or cavity.
  • 灌而掘之 - They poured water and dug it out; '灌' is to pour water; '掘' is to dig.
  • 病尋愈 - The illness soon recovered; '尋' means soon or shortly after.
  • 斯真陰賊之尤者 - This is truly the worst of hidden villains; '陰賊' refers to secret or hidden thieves or villains; '尤者' means 'the worst of.'
  • 文壽 - A name; '文' is likely a surname; '壽' is the given name; structure is 'Wen Shou.'
  • 累舉不第 - Repeatedly failed the examinations; '舉' refers to the imperial examination; '不第' means failing to pass.
  • 其仲弟某者，一戰而捷 - His second brother, someone, won in one battle; implies the second brother succeeded in the exams or in some competition.
  • 父謂壽不學，責而逐之 - His father said Wen Shou did not study, and rebuked and expelled him; '責' means to reprimand; '逐' means to drive away or expel.
  • 母亦時時挫辱其婦 - His mother also constantly belittled his wife; '挫辱' means to humiliate or belittle.
  • 婦故賢，終無怨色 - The wife was virtuous, and never showed resentment; '故' here means 'originally' or 'indeed'; '終無怨色' means 'never showed a face of resentment.'
  • 卿年少，宜自為計 - You are young, you should make your own plans; '卿' is a term of address, similar to 'you' or 'my dear.'
  • 君才妾所知，何有于一第？ - Your talent is known to me, what is one examination to you? A rhetorical question expressing the wife’s confidence in her husband’s abilities.
  • 然得罪堂上，復何言！ - But having offended the elders, what more can be said? '堂上' refers to the elders or parents; '復何言' is a rhetorical expression meaning 'what more can be said?'
  • 幸早有以慰之，妾之願也，敢有他志！ - If only you could soon find a way to console them, that would be my wish; I dare not have other thoughts! A statement of loyalty and determination.
  • 行無資 - He had no funds; '行' here means to travel or go; '無資' means without money.
  • 婦盡取衣飾付質庫 - The wife took all her clothes and jewelry and pawned them; '質庫' is a pawnshop or place where items are pledged for loans.
  • 為戒行李，使應京兆舉 - To provide funds for his travel, so that he could take the examination in Jingzhao; '京兆' is the name of a region or capital; '舉' refers to the imperial examination.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 壁虱 - Can also be used metaphorically to refer to hidden dangers or pests, both literally and figuratively.
  • 某氏女子 - Standard way of referring to an unnamed woman from a certain family; common in classical Chinese narratives.
  • 夢黑甲人為祟 - Dreaming of a supernatural being in armor is a common motif in Chinese folklore, often symbolizing a ghost or spirit causing harm.
  • 日就赢尪 - Medical in nature, indicating a worsening condition; reflects classical Chinese medical terminology.
  • 夜燭之 - Using a lamp to inspect a person is a common diagnostic method in traditional Chinese medicine and folklore, often associated with supernatural investigations.
  • 京兆舉 - Refers to the imperial examination (科舉) held in the Jingzhao region; '京兆' was the name of a district in the capital.
  • 累舉不第 - Reflects the imperial examination system (科舉), which was the primary method of selecting officials in imperial China; failing the examination repeatedly was a common source of personal and familial shame.
  • 文壽 - Likely a fictional or historical figure; used to represent a character in a narrative.
  • 仲弟 - The 'second brother' (仲弟) refers to the second son in a family, following the traditional Chinese order of brothers (伯, 仲, 叔, 季).
  • 質庫 - A pawnshop or a place where goods are pledged for loans; reflects economic practices of the time.
  • 京兆 - Refers to a district in the capital city (probably Chang'an or Luoyang); was the location of the imperial examination.

  • 母怒，抚仲子而笞寿之子 - Means 'the mother became angry, comforted the younger brother's son while beating the elder brother's son.'
  • 垂涕长跪谢 - Means 'shed tears and knelt for a long time to apologize.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 仲弟 - Refers to the younger brother, with '仲' indicating the second-born.
  • 春官试 - Refers to the spring examination, part of the imperial examination system, which was a crucial pathway for social mobility in ancient China.
  • 南宫 - Refers to the imperial examination hall, where the exams were held.
  • 部曹 - Refers to officials in the Ministry of Personnel, a key government department.

  • 末龀 - Refers to a very young age, specifically before the age of eight when a child's teeth have not yet fully grown in.
  • 不栉不沐 - Means 'he does not comb his hair or bathe,' indicating a disregard for personal hygiene.
  • 敝衣一袭，常服之 - Means 'he wears a single tattered robe, which he always wears.' This suggests a life of simplicity or poverty.
  • 冬不裘，夏不葛 - Means 'he does not wear fur in winter, and he does not wear light clothing in summer,' indicating a lack of concern for seasonal changes.
  • 住无常所 - Means 'he has no fixed place to live,' suggesting a nomadic or transient lifestyle.
  • 不常饮食 - Means 'he does not eat regularly,' indicating irregular eating habits.
  • 所行类有道，又类依隐玩世者 - Means 'his actions are similar to those of a person who follows the Way, and also similar to those of a person who lives in seclusion and plays with the world.' '有道' (you dao) refers to someone who follows the moral or philosophical path, while '依隐玩世' (yi yin wan shi) refers to someone who lives in seclusion but still engages with the world in a playful or detached manner.
  • 奇而尾之 - Means 'he found him strange and followed him,' indicating curiosity and observation.
  • 蔽门 - Means 'he hid behind the door,' suggesting stealth or secrecy.
  • 隙窥之 - Means 'he peered through a crack,' indicating a hidden or cautious observation.
  • 土偶见生，皆起迓 - Means 'the clay figurines saw him and all stood up to greet him,' suggesting a supernatural or mystical encounter.
  • 生与抗礼 - Means 'he treated him as an equal,' indicating mutual respect or parity.
  • 坐石上共语 - Means 'they sat on a stone and talked together,' suggesting a casual or informal conversation.
  • 为洸洋恣肆之言甚隐跃 - Means 'he spoke in expansive and unrestrained words, which were very obscure or enigmatic.' '洸洋恣肆' (guang yang zi si) refers to speech that is free-flowing and unrestrained, while '隐跃' (yin yue) means 'obscure' or 'enigmatic.'
  • 或可闻，或不可闻，或可解，或不可解 - Means 'some things could be heard, some could not; some things could be understood, some could not,' indicating a mix of clarity and obscurity in the conversation.
  • 始知生非真哑者 - Means 'he began to realize that the person was not truly mute,' suggesting that the person's silence was not due to a physical inability to speak.
  • 大讶趋归 - Means 'he was greatly surprised and hurried back home,' indicating a strong emotional reaction and immediate action.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 平阳 - A historical region in northern China, known for its cultural and historical significance.
  • 清门 - Refers to a family of high moral standing, often associated with Confucian ideals of virtue and integrity.

  • 凌夷 - Refers to a decline in status or condition, often used to describe the fall of a once-great family or dynasty.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 三日，至一所，其郊原如绨锦，城郭如云霞，宫室如珠贝 - This sequence sets the scene and must be read as a continuous description.
  • 入焉，见其人皆衣绣而冠玉，餐香而饮雪 - This clause describes the people encountered, with the descriptive phrases modifying '其人.'
  • 翠竹蔽其墉，瑶草环其阶，绎花拂其檐，孔翠鸾鹤舞于庭户之间 - This long sequence of descriptive phrases must be read as a single, cohesive description of the surroundings.
  • 无风而神籁韵于耳者，笙璈琴管之音也 - This clause explains the source of the sounds heard, with the descriptive phrase '无风而神籁韵于耳者' leading to the explanation.
  • 旗旌摇摇，帘幞垂垂 - This parallel structure must be read as a single, cohesive description of the visual elements.
  • 栏楯纵横，窗扉四开 - This parallel structure must be read as a single, cohesive description of the architectural elements.
  • 彝鼎几研之属，陈不一处 - This clause describes the arrangement of ritual objects and writing implements, with the descriptive phrase '陈不一处' indicating their distribution.
  • 士人欣然武其后，历数闼，曲折洞达 - This clause describes the scholar's actions and the path he follows, with the descriptive phrase '曲折洞达' indicating the path's complexity and openness.
  • 行其左，奉佛之堂寂以高，经其右，祀仙之馆净以广，升其中，庋书之阁光明而邃奥 - This sequence describes the different halls and buildings encountered, with each descriptive phrase modifying the respective building.
  • 进抵其内，有巨人焉，坐圆台之上 - This clause introduces the giant, with the descriptive phrase '坐圆台之上' indicating his position.
  • 士人敬且畏，仰而瞻，俯而拜，旁而伺，就而问；巨人若弗见焉、弗闻焉，不言不动，寂然如止水。槁然如枯木，屹然若顽石 - This long sequence describes the scholar's actions and the giant's stillness, with the descriptive phrases '寂然如止水，槁然如枯木，屹然若顽石' emphasizing the giant's immobility.
  • 俄有捧衣进履，陈牛羊、具酒浆而进者，巨人如故也 - This clause describes the arrival of servants and the giant's continued stillness, with the descriptive phrase '巨人如故也' indicating his unchanged state.

  • 乐于三山 - Means 'happy in the three mountains' (a metaphor for seclusion and peace).
  • 忠孝之流 - Refers to those who practice loyalty and filial piety.
  • 长于四海 - Means 'spread throughout the four seas.'
  • 菽粟迷味 - Means 'grains and beans are sufficient for taste' (a metaphor for contentment with simple pleasures).
  • 胜于丹药 - Means 'better than elixirs' (a metaphor for natural contentment over artificial means).
  • 忘身而利物 - Means 'forget oneself to benefit others.'
  • 遁世而全己 - Means 'retreat from the world to preserve oneself.'
  • 姑出于此 - Means 'temporarily come out of this state.'
  • 君何羡焉 - Means 'why do you envy this?'
  • 厌薄樊网 - Means 'tired of the cage-like world' (metaphor for worldly constraints).
  • 饬而躬 - Means 'rectify oneself.'
  • 一而志 - Means 'focus one's will.'
  • 凝而神 - Means 'concentrate one's spirit.'
  • 遵理而违欲 - Means 'follow reason and resist desire.'
  • 葆真而适道 - Means 'preserve one's true nature and conform to the Way.'
  • 安在其不仙也 - Means 'how is it not possible to become a sage?'
  • 敬扫白云，待君促坐 - Means 'I respectfully sweep away the white clouds, waiting for you to sit down.'
  • 区区之意，尽于此矣 - Means 'my humble intentions are fully expressed here.'
  • 惘然而登 - Means 'dazedly climb up.'
  • 故舟也 - Means 'the old boat.'
  • 舟为疾风驱至此 - Means 'the boat was driven here by strong winds.'
  • 鼓棹而西 - Means 'paddle westward.'
  • 已达淮泗矣 - Means 'already reached Huai and Si rivers.'
  • 遂归洛中 - Means 'then returned to Luoyang.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 三山 - Refers to the three mythical mountains of immortals, often used to symbolize seclusion and peace.
  • 四海 - Refers to the four seas, symbolizing the vastness of the world.
  • 丹药 - Refers to elixirs of immortality, often associated with Daoist practices.
  • 名教之地 - Refers to the realm of Confucian moral and social norms.
  • 忠孝之流 - Refers to those who practice loyalty and filial piety, core values in Confucianism.
  • 菽粟迷味 - Refers to grains and beans, symbolizing simple and contented living.
  • 忘身而利物 - Refers to the Confucian ideal of selflessness and benefiting others.
  • 遁世而全己 - Refers to the Daoist ideal of retreating from the world to preserve oneself.
  • 葆真而适道 - Refers to the Daoist ideal of preserving one's true nature and conforming to the Way.

Proper Nouns

  • 冯 - Refers to Feng, the listener.
  • 琇 - Refers to Xiu, the speaker.
  • 洛中 - Refers to Luoyang, a city in Henan Province.
  • 淮泗 - Refers to the Huai and Si rivers, important waterways in ancient China.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 西坡逸叟 - A compound name meaning 'the Recluse of Xibo Hill.' '逸叟' is a classical term for a retired or reclusive elder, often used in stories of immortals or supernatural beings.
  • 渭河狐仙 - 'A fox spirit of the Wei River.' '狐仙' is a term for a fox spirit or fox demon, often associated with supernatural abilities and transformation in Chinese folklore.
  • 元時隱山中 - 'In the Yuan dynasty, he lived in seclusion in the mountains.' '隱山中' is a common classical expression for living in seclusion or hermitage.
  • 至洪武己巳 - 'In the year Ji Si of the Hongwu era.' '洪武' refers to the reign of the first Ming emperor, Zhu Yuanzhang. '己巳' is a stem-branch calendar year, corresponding to 1379 AD.
  • 采藥山陽 - 'He was gathering herbs on the sunny side of the mountain.' '山陽' refers to the southern slope of a mountain, which is traditionally considered warmer and more favorable for medicinal herbs.
  • 黑風起艮方 - 'A black wind arose from the Geng direction.' '艮方' is one of the eight trigrams in the *I Ching*, corresponding to the northeast. In classical Chinese, the cardinal directions are often associated with the trigrams.
  • 須臾四障 - 'In a moment, all four directions were blocked.' '四障' may refer to a sudden, all-encompassing obstruction, possibly from a supernatural event.
  • 天地昏黝 - 'The sky and earth turned dark and murky.' A classical expression for a sudden, ominous change in the atmosphere.
  • 秽气扑鼻 - 'A foul smell filled the nose.' A direct translation, but '秽气' is often used in classical texts to describe unnatural or supernatural stench.
  • 少焉，天宇寥青 - 'A little while later, the sky became clear and blue.' '寥青' is a classical term for a clear, bright blue sky.
  • 红云绚晚 - 'Red clouds illuminated the evening.' A poetic way of describing a colorful sunset or an unnatural, magical sky.
  • 恍惚有物，露爪弩空 - 'Vaguely, something appeared, revealing a claw and shooting an arrow into the air.' '露爪' suggests a sudden appearance of a claw, and '弩空' may refer to a bow or crossbow shot into the air.
  • 巨声雷吼 - 'A loud sound like thunder roared.' A metaphorical description of a powerful, unnatural noise.
  • 振动林野 - 'Shaking the forest and fields.' A poetic way of describing a powerful tremor.
  • 叟乃悚恐，疾走还山 - 'The old man was terrified and quickly ran back to the mountain.' '悚恐' is a classical expression for being filled with fear.
  • 回望西方，浓云复布 - 'Looking back westward, thick clouds gathered again.' '浓云复布' suggests a return of ominous weather.
  • 猛雨继集 - 'Heavy rain followed.' '继集' is a classical expression for a continuous or sudden accumulation.
  • 雷訇电烁 - 'Thunder roared and lightning flashed.' '訇' is an onomatopoeic term for loud, echoing thunder.
  • 冰雹凌风 - 'Hailstones flew through the wind.' A vivid description of a storm with hail.
  • 山谷喧撼 - 'The valleys echoed with commotion.' '喧撼' implies a loud, shaking disturbance.
  • 复觉秽气扑鼻如前 - 'He again felt the foul smell as before.' '复觉' emphasizes a recurrence of a sensation.
  • 叟颇遑遽，倥偬斜行 - 'The old man was greatly alarmed and hurriedly moved sideways.' '遑遽' is a classical term for being in a state of panic or agitation. '倥偬' suggests hurried or hasty movement.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 西坡逸叟 - The name of the protagonist, a reclusive figure, likely a human or a supernatural being. The name '逸叟' is a classical way of referring to a hermit or recluse.
  • 渭河 - The Wei River, a major river in northern China, historically significant in Chinese culture and mythology.
  • 狐仙 - A fox spirit, a common figure in Chinese folklore, often associated with transformation, intelligence, and supernatural powers.
  • 元时 - Refers to the Yuan dynasty (1271–1368), a Mongol-led dynasty in China.
  • 洪武己巳 - The year 1379 during the reign of the first Ming emperor, Zhu Yuanzhang. '洪武' is the era name of his reign, and '己巳' is a traditional Chinese calendar year.
  • 山阳 - The southern slope of a mountain, often considered a favorable location for medicinal herbs and cultivation.
  • 艮方 - One of the eight trigrams in the *I Ching*, corresponding to the northeast direction. In classical Chinese cosmology, the trigrams are associated with directions, elements, and natural phenomena.
  • 四障 - The four directions being blocked, possibly a metaphor for a supernatural or mystical event.
  • 天地昏黝 - A description of a darkened sky and earth, often associated with omens or supernatural occurrences.
  • 秽气 - A foul or unnatural smell, often linked to demons, spirits, or otherworldly forces in classical Chinese texts.
  • 红云绚晚 - A poetic description of the sky, possibly indicating a magical or divine presence.
  • 露爪弩空 - A sudden appearance of a claw and a projectile, suggesting the presence of a supernatural creature or spirit.
  • 巨声雷吼 - A loud, thunderous sound, possibly indicating a divine or supernatural event.
  • 振动林野 - A tremor or shaking of the natural world, often used to describe the power of a supernatural force.
  • 悚恐 - A classical term for being terrified or in a state of fear.
  • 回望西方 - Looking back toward the west, possibly symbolizing a return or a direction of significance.
  • 浓云复布 - Thick clouds gathering again, possibly indicating a recurrence of an ominous event.
  • 猛雨继集 - Heavy rain following, suggesting a continuation of a storm or supernatural phenomenon.
  • 雷訇电烁 - Thunder and lightning, often used to describe the power of the heavens or a supernatural force.
  • 冰雹凌风 - Hailstones flying through the wind, indicating a violent storm or supernatural event.
  • 山谷喧撼 - The valleys shaking or echoing with commotion, suggesting a powerful, unnatural disturbance.
  • 复觉秽气扑鼻如前 - A recurrence of the foul smell, reinforcing the supernatural nature of the event.
  • 遑遽 - A state of panic or alarm, often used in classical texts to describe a person's emotional reaction to an extraordinary event.
  • 倥偬斜行 - Hurried, hasty movement, possibly indicating the urgency of the situation.

Proper Nouns

  • 西坡逸叟 - A proper name, the title of the protagonist. Should be translated as 'Xibo Recluse' or 'The Recluse of Xibo.'
  • 渭河 - The Wei River, a proper noun referring to a real river in China. Should be translated as 'Wei River.'

  • 未及对 - Means 'had not yet answered,' indicating a quick exchange.
  • 即手中擘一小桃枝 - Means 'immediately broke off a small peach branch from his hand,' describing the Taoist's action.

  • 此可当兵 - Means 'This can serve as a weapon,' indicating the transformative nature of the peach branch.

  • 视桃枝已化铁矛 - Means 'looked and saw the peach branch had turned into an iron spear,' describing the transformation.
  • 六叉犀利 - Means 'six-pronged and sharp,' detailing the spear's appearance.
  • 寒威可栗 - Means 'its cold presence was terrifying,' emphasizing the spear's power.

  • 欻有一蛙 - Means 'suddenly there appeared a frog,' describing the sudden appearance of the frog.

  • 举爪向叟 - Means 'raised its claws toward the old man,' showing the frog's aggressive intent.
  • 叟即挥矛当之 - Means 'the old man immediately struck it with the spear,' describing the old man's action.
  • 蛙被创脑裂 - Means 'the frog was wounded and its brain split,' detailing the result of the strike.
  • 血膏涂地 - Means 'blood and gore covered the ground,' emphasizing the intensity of the scene.
  • 颓倦 - Means 'exhausted and weary,' describing the old man's state.

  • 乃见前道士骑龙按虎而来 - Means 'then he saw the previous Taoist riding a dragon and taming a tiger,' describing the Taoist's arrival.
  • 谓叟曰 - Means 'said to the old man,' introducing the Taoist's words.
  • 余，孙真人也 - Means 'I am Master Sun,' identifying the Taoist.
  • 今来贺尔 - Means 'I have come to congratulate you,' expressing the Taoist's purpose.
  • 已闻异音仙籁 - Means 'already heard strange, divine sounds,' describing the atmosphere.

  • 道士又遗一葫芦 - Means 'the Taoist also left a gourd,' describing the Taoist's gift.

  • 凌云摄虚 - Means 'soaring through the clouds and void,' describing the Taoist's departure.

  • 又于身边得黄纸 - Means 'also found yellow paper beside himself,' describing the discovery.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 道士 - Refers to a Taoist priest or practitioner of Taoism.

  • 铁矛 - An iron spear, a common weapon in ancient China.
  • 龙 - Dragons are mythical creatures in Chinese culture, often associated with power and good fortune.
  • 虎 - Tigers are powerful animals in Chinese culture, often associated with strength and courage.
  • 癰缯 - A piece of silk, often used in traditional Chinese medicine and rituals.

  • 黄纸 - Yellow paper, often used in traditional Chinese rituals and inscriptions.
  • 助天仙翁 - 'Helper of the Heavenly Immortal Old Man,' a title that suggests a high level of spiritual achievement.
  • 杀蛙之功 - 'Achievement in killing the frog,' which is a metaphor for overcoming obstacles or evil.

Proper Nouns

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 叟自是服丹勤修 - The old man began taking elixirs and diligently practicing spiritual cultivation. '自是' means 'from then on' or 'since then.'
  • 遂登仙箓 - '遂' means 'thus' or 'therefore,' and '登仙箓' means 'ascended to the list of immortals' or 'became an immortal.'
  • 某贵家扶鸾 - '扶鸾' refers to a form of divination where a medium communicates with spirits through a pendulum or similar device. '某贵家' means 'a certain noble family.'
  • 叟降坛自述颠末如此 - '降坛' means 'descended to the altar' or 'appeared on the altar,' and '自述颠末' means 'narrated the entire process' or 'described the whole story.'
  • 主人录其语 - '录其语' means 'recorded his words' or 'wrote down what he said.'
  • 余为诠次焉 - '诠次' means 'arranged' or 'organized,' and '焉' is a particle that can be translated as 'thus' or 'thereby.'
  • 劳生者，好与物交 - '劳生者' means 'those who are weary of life' or 'those who are busy with life,' and '好与物交' means 'like to interact with things' or 'enjoy interacting with objects.'
  • 尝谓交物莫妙于鸡，莫凶于犬 - '尝谓' means 'once said' or 'used to say,' '莫妙于' means 'nothing is better than,' and '莫凶于' means 'nothing is worse than.'
  • 昔尝交犬，其阴如炙，大病数月，服参蓍乃愈 - '昔尝交犬' means 'once interacted with a dog,' '其阴如炙' means 'its yin energy was like roasting,' '大病数月' means 'suffered a serious illness for several months,' and '服参蓍乃愈' means 'took ginseng and mugwort to recover.'
  • 真畏途也 - '真畏途也' means 'truly a fearful path' or 'a truly frightening route.'
  • 由是出犬而宠鸡 - '由是' means 'therefore' or 'as a result,' '出犬' means 'got rid of the dog,' and '宠鸡' means 'treasured the chicken.'
  • 其他羽蹄雌牝，多充下陈 - '其他羽蹄雌牝' means 'other birds and animals, male and female,' and '多充下陈' means 'mostly filled the lower ranks' or 'often occupied the lower positions.'
  • 尝欲汙象，见其庞然大，逡巡退去，象得以贞 - '尝欲汙象' means 'once tried to defile an elephant,' '见其庞然大' means 'saw it was huge,' '逡巡退去' means 'hesitated and retreated,' and '象得以贞' means 'the elephant remained chaste.'
  • 癞虾蟆，蛙属之至陋者也 - '癞虾蟆' means 'toad,' '蛙属之至陋者也' means 'the most ugly among the frog family.'
  • 然其为用，能啖蜈蚣而囚蚊 - '然其为用' means 'but in terms of its function,' '能啖蜈蚣' means 'can eat centipedes,' and '囚蚊' means 'trap mosquitoes.'
  • 不可以貌废也 - '不可以貌废也' means 'cannot be discarded because of its appearance.'
  • 张口向蜈蚣，蜈蚣伏不动，徐徐入其口中，而出于尻窍 - '张口向蜈蚣' means 'opened its mouth towards the centipede,' '蜈蚣伏不动' means 'the centipede lay still,' '徐徐入其口中' means 'slowly entered its mouth,' and '而出于尻窍' means 'and came out through its anus.'
  • 复回身张口向中，又出入如前者三四，遂葬其腹矣 - '复回身张口向中' means 'turned around and opened its mouth again,' '又出入如前者三四' means 'repeated the process several times,' and '遂葬其腹矣' means 'and finally buried it in its belly.'
  • 咀嚼之法，未有奇于此者 - '咀嚼之法' means 'method of chewing,' and '未有奇于此者' means 'there is nothing more extraordinary than this.'
  • 既啖数头，乃强饲以丹砂，缄其口而倒悬之，以盘承其涎 - '既啖数头' means 'after eating several,' '乃强饲以丹砂' means 'then forced to feed it cinnabar,' '缄其口而倒悬之' means 'sealed its mouth and hung it upside down,' and '以盘承其涎' means 'caught its saliva in a tray.'
  • 既尽，放去，取笔濡诞，画圈于纸，着壁上，室中之蚊咸集圈中 - '既尽' means 'after it was done,' '放去' means 'released it,' '取笔濡诞' means 'took a brush and dipped it in the saliva,' '画圈于纸' means 'drew a circle on the paper,' '着壁上' means 'on the wall,' and '室中之蚊咸集圈中' means 'all the mosquitoes in the room gathered in the circle.'
  • 去其圈，蚊复飞去 - '去其圈' means 'removed the circle,' and '蚊复飞去' means 'the mosquitoes flew away again.'

  • 若必毋出 - Another formal prohibition, meaning 'you must not go out.'
